title: Synsepalum brenanii
intro: Synsepalum brenanii is a plant species of the family Sapotaceae, native to West Africa, known for its unique properties as a natural sweetener. The health benefits of Synsepalum brenanii include its ability to suppress the sensation of sweetness, making it a useful tool for weight loss and diabetes management. The bioactive constituents of this plant include synsepalan, a glycoside responsible for its unique sweet suppression properties. Synsepalum brenanii is commonly prepared into herbal extracts, capsules, and teas, which are used to support weight loss and glucose regulation in traditional African medicine.
taxonomy: Synsepalum brenanii is classified within the family Sapotaceae. This family includes over 100 genera of tropical flowering plants commonly known as sapotes. The genus Synsepalum is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. It is closely related to the genus Pouteria, which also belongs to the Sapotaceae family. Synsepalum brenanii is one of the several species within the Synsepalum genus.
